questions.q: Who can formally introduce a bill in Congress?
questions.explanation: Ideas can come from anyone, but only a member of Congress introduces a bill.

questions.q: Where do most bills go to be studied and often die?
questions.explanation: Most bills never make it out of committee.

questions.q: Before going to the President, a bill must be passed by which chambers?
options: Both the House and the Senate in the same form
questions.explanation: Both chambers must pass identical text.

questions.q: What can the President do with a bill?
options: Sign it into law or veto it
questions.explanation: The President signs or vetoes; a veto can be overridden.

questions.q: How can a bill become law even if the President vetoes it?
options: Congress overrides the veto with a two-thirds vote in each chamber
questions.explanation: A two-thirds vote in both chambers overrides the veto.
